title: Phase 3: Operations
description: Day-to-day session management: restart stuck agents and generate morning standups from beads.
acceptance_criteria: initech restart eng1 --bead <id> kills the window, starts a new one with Claude, and dispatches the bead. initech standup prints shipped/active/next from beads.

title: Implement cmd/restart command
description: Kill and restart a specific agent with optional bead re-dispatch. Combines stop + start in one command. Uses --continue fallback pattern. The key difference from stop+start: restart always uses --continue first (crashed agent should resume), and includes the 5s delay + bead dispatch in one flow.
design: cmd/restart.go with cobra command. Takes single role arg + optional --bead flag. Calls tmux.KillWindow, tmux.NewWindow, sleep 500ms, tmux.SendKeys with startup command, sleep 5s if --bead, tmux.SendKeys with dispatch.
acceptance_criteria: Kills the agent's tmux window and creates a new one. Starts Claude with the --continue fallback pattern and correct permission tier. Optional --bead flag dispatches after 5s delay. Validates role exists in config. Error if session not running.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Kill + new-window + 500ms sleep + send-keys with --continue fallback. --bead flag dispatches after 5s. Validates role, checks session exists. KillWindow error ignored (window may not exist).

title: Implement cmd/standup command
description:
Generate a morning standup from beads. Shells out to bd for recently closed, in-progress, and ready beads. Formats as markdown.

Flow (per systemdesign 4.6):
1. Load initech.yaml
2. Calculate yesterday's date
3. Run bd list queries (graceful if bd not available):
   - Recently closed (shipped since yesterday)
   - In progress (active work)
   - Ready (next up, limit 5)
4. Format and print standup

Output format:
  ## <project> Daily - 2026-02-15
  ### What's New
  - <id>: <title> (shipped)
  ### In Progress
  - <id>: <title> (<assignee>)
  ### Next Up
  - <id>: <title>
design: cmd/standup.go with cobra command. Uses exec.Runner to call bd list with --json. Parses JSON output for bead ID, title, status, assignee. Time calculation uses time.Now().AddDate(0,0,-1) for yesterday.
acceptance_criteria: Prints formatted standup with three sections. Handles missing bd gracefully. Shows assignee for in-progress beads. Limits next-up to 5. Date header uses today's date.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Queries bd for closed/in_progress/ready beads. Three sections: What's New (shipped, limit 10), In Progress (with assignee), Next Up (ready, limit 5). Graceful when bd not installed.

title: Phase 4: Content
description: All 11 role templates production quality. Bootstrap produces CLAUDE.md files that drive correct agent behavior without manual editing.
acceptance_criteria: initech init with all 11 roles produces role-specific CLAUDE.md files. Each template covers: identity, critical failure modes, decision authority, constraints, artifacts, workflow, communication.

title: Implement remaining role templates
description: Add PM, Arch, Sec, Shipper, PMM, Writer, Ops, Growth templates. Update scaffold templateForRole to route all 11 roles correctly.
acceptance_criteria: All 11 roles get role-specific templates. Scaffold routes each role to the correct template. Tests verify all templates render and contain expected sections.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Added PM, Arch, Sec, Shipper, PMM, Writer, Ops, Growth templates. Updated scaffold templateForRole to route all 11 roles (plus numbered variants like eng1, qa2). 8 new template tests.

title: Phase 1: Foundation
description: initech init + initech up work end-to-end. A user can bootstrap a project and start a tmux session with agents from a single config file.
acceptance_criteria: Run initech init in a fresh directory with a valid initech.yaml, then initech up. A tmux session starts with super and eng1 windows, each running Claude with correct permissions and CLAUDE.md files. Manual verification by Nelson.

title: Implement internal/exec package
description: Create the exec.Runner interface and DefaultRunner implementation. This is the leaf dependency that every other package uses to shell out to external commands (tmux, git, bd, tmuxinator). Wraps os/exec with consistent error handling. Must support Run(name, args) and RunInDir(dir, name, args).
design: Single file exec.go with interface + default impl. Test file exec_test.go verifies real command execution (echo, ls) and error cases (nonexistent binary). No external dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Runner interface defined with Run() and RunInDir() methods. DefaultRunner implementation shells out via os/exec. All tests pass. Package doc comment explains purpose and concurrency safety.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Implemented exec.Runner interface with DefaultRunner (os/exec) and FakeRunner (test helper). 7 tests: Run, RunInDir, trim, empty dir, nonexistent binary, non-zero exit, FakeRunner recording. go vet clean.

title: Implement cmd/up command
description: Wire up the initech up command. Thin wrapper around tmuxinator start. Loads config, verifies tmuxinator config exists, runs tmuxinator start <project>. Reports session name and agent count on success.
design: cmd/up.go with cobra command. Load config, check for tmuxinator YAML, exec tmuxinator start via exec.Runner. Count roles from config for the success message.
acceptance_criteria: initech up loads initech.yaml, verifies ~/.config/tmuxinator/<project>.yml exists, runs tmuxinator start. Prints 'Session <name> started with N agents.' on success. Error if no initech.yaml found. Error if tmuxinator config missing (suggest running initech init).

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. cmd/up loads config via Discover, verifies tmuxinator YAML exists, runs tmuxinator start, prints agent count. Error messages guide user to initech init.

title: Implement internal/config package
description: Create the config types (Project, Repo, BeadsConfig, RoleOverride), Load() to read initech.yaml, and Validate() to enforce constraints. This package owns the initech.yaml schema. Does not know about files on disk, tmux, or git beyond reading its own config file.
design: config.go with types + Load + Validate. Config discovery: --config flag, ./initech.yaml, walk upward. Use yaml.v3 for unmarshaling. Test with table-driven tests covering valid configs, missing fields, invalid references.
acceptance_criteria: Go types match systemdesign section 2.2. Load() reads YAML from path, returns Project. Validate() checks: project required, root required and expandable, roles non-empty, grid subset of roles, role_overrides keys in roles. All tests pass including validation edge cases.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Config types, Load, Discover (walk-upward), Validate, Write, expandHome. 14 tests covering valid config, missing fields, duplicate roles, grid/override validation, roundtrip write, home expansion.

title: Implement internal/roles/catalog package
description: Create the role catalog: RoleDef type, PermissionTier enum, the Catalog map of all 11 well-known roles, and LookupRole() function. Open catalog design: unknown role names return a sensible default (Autonomous, no src, no playbooks).
design: catalog.go with types and map. Single LookupRole function. No dependencies on other internal packages. Test verifies each catalog entry has correct permission tier and flags.
acceptance_criteria: RoleDef struct with Name, Permission, NeedsSrc, NeedsPlaybooks, NeedsMakefile, Template fields. All 11 roles defined per systemdesign section 3.1. LookupRole returns catalog entry for known roles, default for unknown. Tests cover all catalog entries and unknown role fallback.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. 13 roles in catalog with correct permission tiers and flags. LookupRole with open catalog (unknown roles get Autonomous defaults). 8 tests covering completeness, tiers, src/playbooks/makefile flags, name consistency, known/unknown lookup.

title: Implement internal/roles/render package
description: Create the template rendering system: RenderVars struct, Render() function with regex-based {{variable}} substitution. Matches the beads pattern. Unknown variables left as-is. No conditionals or loops.
design: render.go with compiled regex pattern, RenderVars struct, Render function. Pattern: regexp.MustCompile(\{\{(\w+)\}\}). Map-based lookup from struct fields. No text/template dependency.
acceptance_criteria: RenderVars struct with ProjectName, ProjectRoot, RepoURL, TechStack, BuildCmd, TestCmd, BeadsPrefix. Render() substitutes all known variables, leaves unknown ones intact. No runtime errors on missing vars. Tests cover: full substitution, partial vars, unknown vars preserved, empty string vars.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Render() with regex {{variable}} substitution and RenderString() convenience. 7 tests: full, partial, unknown preserved, no vars, all vars, multiple occurrences, RenderString.

title: Implement internal/roles/templates (super + eng only)
description: Create the first two role templates as inline Go string constants: SuperTemplate and EngTemplate. Also create the four document templates (PRDTemplate, SpecTemplate, SystemDesignTemplate, RoadmapTemplate). These prove the template pattern works before building all 11 role templates in Phase 4.
design: templates.go with const SuperTemplate, const EngTemplate. doctemplates.go with const PRDTemplate, SpecTemplate, SystemDesignTemplate, RoadmapTemplate. Content follows spec section 2 role definitions and systemdesign section 3.4/3.5. Test verifies each template renders without error and contains expected section headers.
acceptance_criteria: SuperTemplate covers: identity, critical failure modes, decision authority, responsibilities, constraints, artifacts, workflow, communication, cross-role boundaries. EngTemplate covers the same structure for engineers. Four document templates match systemdesign section 3.5. All templates use {{variable}} placeholders that render.Render() can substitute. Templates are 100-200 lines each for roles, 30-60 lines each for documents.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. SuperTemplate (~90 lines), EngTemplate (~90 lines), QATemplate (~55 lines). Four doc templates: PRD, Spec, SystemDesign, Roadmap. 7 test cases: render verification, section presence, role_name preservation, Phase 0 content in roadmap.

title: Implement internal/tmuxinator package
description: Generate tmuxinator YAML from an initech config. Produces both main session YAML and grid session YAML as []byte. Does not write files (scaffold does that). Does not know about scaffold, git, or tmux runtime.
design: tmuxinator.go with Generate and GenerateGrid functions. Build YAML structure in Go structs, marshal with yaml.v3. Window startup command: supervised roles get 'claude --continue', autonomous get 'claude --continue --dangerously-skip-permissions'. Depends on config types and roles/catalog for permission tier lookup.
acceptance_criteria: Generate() takes a config.Project and returns main session YAML. GenerateGrid() returns grid session YAML. Output matches the tmuxinator pattern from spec section 1.2: session name, project root, pre_window with BEADS_DIR export, windows with role names, correct startup commands per permission tier. Grid YAML uses layout tiled. Tests verify YAML structure, correct window count, permission-appropriate commands.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Generate() and GenerateGrid() producing tmuxinator YAML. Correct permission-tier commands, pre_window BEADS_DIR, grid naming. 8 tests: structure, window count, permissions, pre_window, empty grid, unknown roles.

title: Implement internal/scaffold package
description: Create the project tree on disk from a config. Creates directories, writes rendered CLAUDE.md files per role, writes root CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .gitignore, docs/ with four document templates. Idempotent: checks os.Stat() before writing, existing files not overwritten unless force=true.
design: scaffold.go with Scaffold(project, runner, force) function. Uses roles/catalog to determine what each role needs (src dir, playbooks dir, makefile). Uses roles/render to substitute template variables. Writes files with os.WriteFile. Tests use t.TempDir() for isolation.
acceptance_criteria: Scaffold() takes config.Project and creates the full directory tree per spec section 4.2. Role directories created with CLAUDE.md from rendered templates. docs/ created with prd.md, spec.md, systemdesign.md, roadmap.md from document templates. Root CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md written. .gitignore written. Existing files preserved unless force flag set. Tests verify directory structure, file contents, idempotency.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Scaffold creates full project tree: root dir, .gitignore, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, docs/ with 4 document templates, role dirs with CLAUDE.md (template-selected per role), .claude/, src/, playbooks/. Idempotent with force override. RoleOverrides applied. 10 tests.

title: Implement internal/git package
description: Shell out to git for repo initialization, submodule management, and commits. Uses exec.Runner for all command execution. Does not know about config or tmux.
design: git.go with Init, AddSubmodule, Commit functions. Each takes an exec.Runner and relevant parameters. Uses RunInDir for directory-scoped operations. Error wrapping with context.
acceptance_criteria: Init() runs git init in a directory (skip if .git exists). AddSubmodule() runs git submodule add. Commit() stages and commits with a message. All functions take exec.Runner as parameter. Tests use a fake Runner to verify correct git commands are invoked without requiring real git.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Init (skip existing), AddSubmodule, CommitAll. All use exec.Runner. 7 tests with FakeRunner verifying correct git commands, directory scoping, error wrapping.

title: Implement cmd/init command
description: Wire up the initech init command. Orchestrates the full bootstrap flow: interactive setup (or load existing initech.yaml), validate config, scaffold project tree, init git, add submodules, init beads, generate tmuxinator configs, initial commit. This is the composition root that connects all internal packages.
design: cmd/init.go with cobra command. Reads or creates config, calls scaffold.Scaffold, git.Init, git.AddSubmodule for each NeedsSrc role, shells out to bd init, writes tmuxinator YAML to ~/.config/tmuxinator/, runs git add + commit. Uses exec.DefaultRunner for real execution.
acceptance_criteria: initech init in an empty directory with interactive prompts creates: initech.yaml, .beads/, .gitignore, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, docs/ with four documents, role directories with CLAUDE.md files, tmuxinator config at ~/.config/tmuxinator/. initech init with existing initech.yaml skips interactive setup. Graceful degradation: skip beads if bd not found, still generate tmuxinator YAML if tmuxinator not installed. Print summary of created files.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. cmd/init orchestrates full bootstrap: load or interactive setup, validate config, scaffold project tree, git init, submodule add, beads init (graceful degradation), tmuxinator YAML generation, initial commit, summary output. Smoke tested end-to-end: initech init with config file creates all expected directories, files, docs, role CLAUDE.md files, tmuxinator config.

title: Phase 2: Visibility and Lifecycle
description: Nelson can see what's happening and manage agent lifecycle without manual tmux commands. Includes status, down, stop/start, and doctor.
acceptance_criteria: initech status shows per-agent Claude detection, bead assignment, and memory usage. initech down warns about uncommitted work. initech stop/start manages individual agents for memory management. initech doctor checks all prerequisites.

title: Implement internal/tmux package
description:
Port tmux interaction from gastools. Session inspection, window listing, Claude detection, process memory measurement, kill/new window, send-keys. Uses exec.Runner for all tmux CLI calls.

Key types:
- Window: Index, Name, PaneID, Command (pane_current_command)
- AgentStatus: one of running/down/stopped

Key functions:
- SessionExists(name) bool
- ListWindows(session) ([]Window, error)
- IsClaudeRunning(Window) bool: detect Claude by pane_current_command (node, claude, version pattern) with child process fallback when pane shows a shell
- GetProcessMemory(Window) (uint64, error): RSS of the Claude process tree in the pane, in bytes. Use pane_pid -> pgrep for claude/node child -> ps rss. Return 0 if no process found.
- KillWindow(session, window) error
- NewWindow(session, window) error
- SendKeys(target, text) error

Port the IsClaudeRunning detection pattern from gastools (gasnudge/internal/tmux/tmux.go:151-175): check pane_current_command for 'node', 'claude', or version pattern; if command is a shell, check for claude/node child processes via pgrep -P.

Memory measurement: given a pane, find the Claude process PID (same detection as IsClaudeRunning but return the PID), then sum RSS for that PID and all descendants. Use ps -o rss= on macOS. Return bytes.
design: tmux.go with Window type and all functions. Each function takes runner as first arg. Claude detection: regex for version pattern, shell list for child process check, pgrep -P for child discovery. Memory: pgrep -P to find claude/node child PID, then ps -o rss= to get RSS. Test file with FakeRunner verifying all tmux command invocations.
acceptance_criteria: SessionExists returns true/false correctly. ListWindows returns all windows with parsed metadata. IsClaudeRunning detects Claude via direct command match and child process fallback. GetProcessMemory returns RSS in bytes for the Claude process tree, 0 when no Claude running. KillWindow/NewWindow/SendKeys shell out correctly. All functions take exec.Runner. Tests use FakeRunner.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Ported from gastools with exec.Runner. SessionExists, ListWindows (5-field format with PanePID), IsClaudeRunning (direct/version/shell-child detection), GetClaudePID, GetProcessMemory (main + children RSS), KillWindow, NewWindow, SendKeys. 17 tests including fakeMultiRunner for memory measurement.

title: Implement cmd/status command
description:
Show running agents, Claude detection, bead assignment, and memory usage per agent. This is the primary visibility tool.

Flow (per systemdesign 4.4):
1. Load initech.yaml
2. Check session exists (if not: 'not running, use initech up')
3. tmux list-windows to get all windows with pane metadata
4. For each window: detect Claude running, get process memory
5. If bd available: bd list --status in_progress --json, match agents to beads by assignee
6. For roles in config not found as windows: mark as 'stopped'
7. Print status table matching PRD journey 5.4:

  Session: project (running, N agents, M stopped, X.X GB total)

    Role      Claude  Bead                              Status          Mem
    super     yes     -                                 -            1.3 GB
    eng1      yes     bb-3fa.5 (Settings dialog)        in_progress  2.1 GB
    qa2       no      -                                 agent down        -
    pm        -       -                                 stopped           -

Status logic:
- Window exists + Claude running + has bead: show bead title and status
- Window exists + Claude running + no bead: 'idle'
- Window exists + Claude not running: 'agent down'
- Window missing from tmux: 'stopped'
- Memory: right-aligned, GB with 1 decimal, dash for stopped/down
design: cmd/status.go with cobra command. Loads config, calls tmux.ListWindows, tmux.IsClaudeRunning, tmux.GetProcessMemory for each. Optionally shells out to bd list --json for bead matching. Formats table with fixed-width columns.
acceptance_criteria: Correctly detects running/down/stopped agents. Shows per-agent memory in GB. Shows total memory in header. If bd available, matches beads to agents by assignee. If bd not available, shows dash for bead column. Roles from config that are missing from tmux show as 'stopped'. Table formatting matches PRD.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Status table with Claude detection, bead matching via bd list --json, per-agent memory (RSS of process tree), total in header. Stopped roles detected by absence from tmux window list. Smoke tested against live 7-agent session.

title: Implement cmd/down command
description:
Stop the tmux session with safety checks for uncommitted work.

Flow (per systemdesign 4.3):
1. Load initech.yaml
2. Check session exists (if not: 'not running')
3. For each role with NeedsSrc (from catalog): run git -C <role>/src status --porcelain
4. If any dirty repos and no --force: print warnings, abort with hint
5. Run tmuxinator stop <project> (with inherited stdio, same pattern as up)
6. If grid session running: tmuxinator stop <project>-grid
7. Print: 'Session stopped.'

The --force flag bypasses uncommitted work warnings.
design: cmd/down.go with cobra command. Uses exec.Runner for git status checks. tmuxinator stop needs inherited stdio (same as up). Uses roles.LookupRole to check NeedsSrc per role.
acceptance_criteria: Warns about uncommitted changes in agent src/ dirs before stopping. Aborts without --force when dirty. Stops both main and grid sessions. Prints which agents have uncommitted work. No-ops gracefully when session not running.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Checks git status --porcelain in each NeedsSrc role's src/ dir. Warns about uncommitted changes, aborts without --force. Stops main + grid sessions via tmuxinator stop with inherited stdio.

title: Implement cmd/stop command
description:
Stop individual agents to free memory without taking down the whole session. Accepts one or more role names.

Flow:
1. Load initech.yaml
2. Validate each role exists in config
3. Check session exists
4. For each role: tmux kill-window -t <project>:<role>
5. Print confirmation per role

initech stop arch sec pm -> stops those three windows. Status command should then show them as 'stopped' (window missing from tmux but present in config).
design: cmd/stop.go with cobra command taking variadic role args. Uses tmux.KillWindow for each. Validates against config roles list.
acceptance_criteria: Stops named agent windows in the tmux session. Multiple roles in one command. Validates roles exist in config. Graceful error if role window already stopped. Prints confirmation per role.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Kills individual tmux windows. Validates roles against config. Multiple roles in one command. Smoke tested: stop pm eng2 correctly removed windows, status showed 'stopped'.

title: Implement cmd/start command
description:
Bring stopped agents back into the session. Accepts one or more role names and optional --bead flag.

Flow:
1. Load initech.yaml
2. Validate each role exists in config
3. Check session exists
4. For each role:
   a. tmux new-window -t <project> -n <role>
   b. Build startup command from catalog (dir + claude --continue + permission flag)
   c. tmux send-keys to start Claude in the new window
   d. If --bead provided: sleep 5s, then gn -w <role> dispatch message
5. Print confirmation per role

The --bead flag dispatches a bead to the agent after startup, with a 5-second delay for Claude to initialize.
design: cmd/start.go with cobra command taking variadic role args and --bead string flag. Uses tmux.NewWindow, tmux.SendKeys. Builds startup command using roles.LookupRole for permission tier. Dispatch uses the gn format from spec section 3.2.
acceptance_criteria: Creates new tmux windows for named roles. Starts Claude with correct permission tier per catalog. Optional --bead flag dispatches bead context via send-keys after 5s delay. Multiple roles in one command. Validates roles exist in config. Graceful error if role window already exists.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Creates new tmux window, sends cd + claude --continue with correct permission tier. --bead flag dispatches after 5s delay. Smoke tested: start pm brought window back, Claude initialized.

title: Implement cmd/doctor command
description:
Check all prerequisites with version, path, and status. Diagnostic tool for installation and mid-session debugging.

Prerequisites to check (8 tools):
1. tmux - required, 'brew install tmux'
2. tmuxinator - required, 'gem install tmuxinator'
3. claude - required, Claude Code CLI
4. git - required, 'brew install git'
5. bd - optional, 'brew tap nmelo/tap && brew install bd'
6. gn - optional, 'brew tap nmelo/tap && brew install gn'
7. gp - optional, 'brew tap nmelo/tap && brew install gp'
8. gm - optional, 'brew tap nmelo/tap && brew install gm'

For each: run 'which <tool>' for path, '<tool> --version' or '<tool> version' for version. Display table:

  tool          version  path                            status
  tmux          3.4      /opt/homebrew/bin/tmux           ok
  tmuxinator    -        -                                MISSING

If any required tools missing: list fix commands grouped by install method.
If all present: 'All prerequisites satisfied. Run initech init to get started.'
design: cmd/doctor.go with cobra command. No dependency on internal/tmux. Uses exec.Runner to shell out to which and version commands. Table formatting. Prerequisite list is a slice of structs with name, required bool, version command, install hint.
acceptance_criteria: Checks all 8 prerequisites. Shows version, path, and ok/MISSING status. Groups fix instructions by install method (brew, gem). Distinguishes required vs optional tools. Exit code 0 when all required tools present, 1 when any required tool missing.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Checks 8 prerequisites (4 required, 4 optional). Shows version, path, status table. Groups fix hints by install method. Exit code 1 when required tools missing.
